title: Thin Scope, Vertical Slices, and Evidence-Driven Roadmap · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs thin scope, vertical slices, and evidence-driven roadmap from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
Slice work across interface, behavior, data, verification, accessibility, security, and operations to produce one end-to-end learning signal.
Order slices by prerequisite knowledge, risk retirement, feedback value, and reversible commitment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Story Outcome · changed case · Review Story Outcome
steps.instruction:
review case — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

Which action demonstrates Story Outcome without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Story Outcome · changed case · Review Story Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. For Story Outcome · changed case · Review Story Outcome, keep Story Outcome connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Story Outcome condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Privacy Framework — Controls purpose, data processing, governance, communication, protection, retention, correction, and privacy-risk reasoning without presenting the draft as final.

Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

For Story Outcome · changed case · Review Story Outcome, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A component or database task is automatically a user story.

options.text: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap.
A component or database task is automatically a user story. That claim does not survive the story outcome · changed case evidence boundary.

stimulus.title: Scope Tradeoff case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Fixed time can be met by temporarily dropping accessibility or testing.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries. Artifact under review: risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Scope Tradeoff, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Scope Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Scope Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Scope Tradeoff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Scope Tradeoff, keep Scope Tradeoff connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.
